mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production index reflects expanding manufacturing output and capacity utilization, driving corporate earnings expectations and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day returns are negative in more than 55% of months when INDPRO is rising month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 2.25%, restrictive monetary policy elevates the risk-free rate above the equity risk premium threshold, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when FEDFUNDS exceeds 2.25%.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous month-over-month decline in industrial production and rise in the unemployment rate signals a contractionary supply-labor shock that depresses corporate revenues and margins, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines and UNRATE rises month-over-month
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day returns are non-negative in more than 50% of triggered instances, or either proxy alone produces stronger directional accuracy than the joint signal.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, a month-over-month increase in nonfarm payrolls exceeding 200 thousand signals an overheating labor market that induces wage-driven margin compression and generates negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances during late-cycle expansions, or the signal fails to outperform baseline in that regime.

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in the consumer price index month-over-month increase signals mounting inflation pressure that raises near-term Treasury yield expectations and drags down S&P 500 prices over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day returns are positive in more than 55% of months when CPIAUCSL is accelerating.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in housing starts signals weakening residential investment and forward-looking demand erosion that precedes negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls month-over-month for two consecutive months
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day returns are non-negative in more than 50% of instances following two consecutive monthly declines in HOUST.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in the federal funds rate and the consumer price index signal a stagflationary regime where monetary tightening coincides with cost pressures, squeezing corporate margins and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises month-over-month and CPIAUCSL rises month-over-month
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the joint signal underperforms the simple average of single-series signals.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales volumes reflect robust consumer spending that directly lifts corporate revenue expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day returns are negative in more than 50% of months when RSAFS is rising month-over-month.
